items.headline: The banking lobby is wrong about stablecoins and community banks
items.subheadline: Congress should not kneecap one of the clearest advances in payment infrastructure to protect community banks from a threat that has not been proven, argues Eco CEO Ryne Saxe.
items.author: Ryne Sax

items.headline: What Robinhood’s recent layoffs say about the current state of crypto investments
items.subheadline: Despite the trading platform’s restructuring and the wave of crypto companies making cuts, signaling a late bear market, Altcoin Pro’s Horst, Anderson and Zhuleku explain there’s no reason to panic.
items.author: Ryan Horst

items.headline: Tokenized securities need competition, not gatekeepers
items.subheadline: Innovation thrives when investors have choices. For tokenized securities, Washington shouldn't pick winners before the market has a chance to learn what works, argues Patrick McHenry, vice chairman at Ondo Finance and former Chairman of the House Financial Services Committee.
items.author: Patrick McHenry

items.headline: Europe is closing the door on offshore crypto, but it’s leaving the riskiest window open
items.subheadline: MiCA was never meant to address the giant crypto derivatives market. That could pose a serious problem, says Patrick Gruhn, founder and chief executive of Perpetuals.com.
items.author: Patrick Gruhn

items.headline: Live markets: Bitcoin rises above $61,000 as U.S. jobs data for June disappoints
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: ChatGPT developer OpenAI reported to discuss offering U.S. government a 5% stake
items.subheadline: Proposal aims to give the public a financial stake in AI while easing political scrutiny of the industry.
items.author: James Van Straten

items.headline: BTC price news: Bitcoin zooms above $61,000 as inflation fears soften
items.subheadline: Bitcoin rose after Fed Chair Kevin Warsh said inflation risks had eased. A 7.9% drop in South Korea's Kospi on renewed AI chip worries did little to dent the bid.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Smaller tokens Memecore's M, Auderia's beat lead as bitcoin, sol rally in 'first real bounce of the selloff'
items.subheadline: Bitcoin and major cryptocurrencies rebounded on dovish Federal Reserve signals, with speculative tokens like Memecore’s M and Audiera’s BEAT leading gains.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Kevin Warsh comments set the stage for nonfarm payrolls data to ignite BTC, gold rally: Crypto Daily
items.subheadline: The day ahead in crypto: July 2, 2026
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: U.S. senators seek to block foreign adversaries from AI technology in new bill
items.subheadline: Senators Tim Scott and Bill Hagerty, who managed to push the crypto GENIUS Act into law, introduced the bill to give the government powers to defend U.S. AI.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Meta is paying creators in Stablecoins. Spending them is someone else's problem
items.subheadline: Meta’s decision to pay creators in USDC validates stablecoins as a mainstream disbursement tool, Joslyn suggests, but it also exposes the industry’s unresolved problem: moving seamlessly from digital dollars to usable local currency.
items.author: Tim Joslyn

items.headline: Vitalik Buterin says crypto’s most powerful idea is still nowhere near ready
items.subheadline: The Ethereum co-founder says indistinguishability obfuscation could one day act like a “trustless trusted third party,” but today’s versions remain far too slow for real use.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Solana launches onchain governance and sets entry fee at 100,000 SOL staked
items.subheadline: Solana has activated a formal on-chain governance system. Here's how it works.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Securitize heads to NYSE debut after investors approve SPAC merger;  CEPT gains 20%
items.subheadline: The tokenization firm is expected to begin trading on the NYSE on Thursday, becoming one of the first publicly traded pure-play tokenization companies.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: New York Life's $800B asset manager makes tokenization debut with Centrifuge fund
items.subheadline: The $807 billion asset manager is bringing a high-yield corporate bond strategy onto blockchain with Centrifuge as Wall Street expands beyond tokenized Treasury funds.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Circle (CRCL) tumbles as Stripe, Coinbase (COIN) and BlackRock (BLK) back rival stablecoin network
items.subheadline: Open Standard's Open USD aims to let partners keep reserve income and eliminate minting fees, challenging Circle's USDC.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Circle (CRCL) selloff may be 'overreaction' but Open USD faces adoption test
items.subheadline: The Stripe- and Coinbase-backed stablecoin consortium can challenge Circle's business model, but analysts say building a network is harder than assembling big-name partners.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Companies spending the most on AI are growing jobs, Ramp study finds
items.subheadline: A new study from Ramp found companies making the biggest investments in AI are expanding their workforces, with heavy adopters increasing headcount by about 10% and entry-level hiring by 12%, challenging fears that generative AI is already causing widespread job losses.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Crypto wallet Phantom pushes deeper into perps hiring team behind Hyperliquid's OpenAI, Anthropic markets
items.subheadline: The crypto wallet is doubling down on perpetual futures after hiring the builders behind one of Hyperliquid's highest-profile market experiments.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: French banking giant Crédit Agricole rolls out euro stablecoin, EURXT
items.subheadline: EURXT launched with 20.02 million tokens in circulation, backed 1:1 by euro reserves at CACEIS Bank, according to data on the project's website.
items.author: Francisco Rodrigues

items.headline: Robinhood (HOOD) rolls out public blockchain as it expands deeper into crypto
items.subheadline: Robinhood's product push continues to blur the line between crypto and more traditional finance.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Taiko fully restores cross-chain bridge just 10 days after a $1.7 million hack
items.subheadline: The protocol completed a multi-stage recovery that included an independent security review. The TAIKO token has surged as much as 136% in recent trading.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Metaplanet (3350) buys another $170 million of bitcoin expanding treasury to 43,000 BTC
items.subheadline: The Japanese firm, now the world's third largest publicly traded bitcoin holder, reported stronger Bitcoin Income Generation revenue.
items.author: James Van Straten

items.headline: Ripple news: XRP Ledger moves to add onchain lending in latest moves
items.subheadline: A proposed XRPL standard would let institutions borrow against tokenized assets, with the blockchain enforcing loan terms while the underwriting stays with human credit teams. It still needs validator approval to go live.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Private keys, not smart contracts, caused 40% of crypto's $16 billion hack losses. Here's whats being done.
items.subheadline: The industry is moving toward fixing the private key vulnerability issue, just not evenly, Wish Wu, co-founder and CEO of Pharos, said.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Live markets: BlackRock's IBIT sheds $300 million as bitcoin demand dwindles
items.subheadline: IBIT alone shed $300 million while smaller funds absorbed some of the outflow. The selloff lands as the same AI trade that crashed Korea a week ago now powers a record quarterly rally there.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: MetaMask launches Money Account with stablecoin yield and spending in one wallet
items.subheadline: The launch reflects a broader push to make stablecoins more useful beyond trading and transfers.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Anthropic restores AI models Fable, Mythos after the U.S. lifts export controls
items.subheadline: The government cleared Fable 5 and Mythos 5 on June 30, weeks after a cybersecurity finding triggered an export order that froze access for everyone.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Live updates: Bitcoin ETFs had their worst month ever in June, shedding $4.5 billion
items.subheadline: Record ETF outflows in June beat the previous worst month by 29% and came on nine consecutive days of redemptions to close the period.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Prices of tokenized Google stock inflated 7,700% in rare DeFi lending exploit
items.subheadline: An attacker inflated the value of a tokenized Google share used as collateral to about 78 times its real price, then borrowed against it, leaving roughly $403,000 in bad debt.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Ethereum (ETH) news: A new nonprofit launches with a focus on Wall Street and institutional adoption
items.subheadline: The launch comes as the Ethereum Foundation narrows its focus to stewarding the core protocol, with independent organizations like EthLabs emerging to take on ecosystem functions such as research & development.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: EthLabs launches as Ethereum undergoes its biggest leadership transition in years
items.subheadline: In this week's edition of The Protocol Newsletter, we’re diving into the creation of EthLabs, and why it was launched during a period of transition for the ecosystem.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Ethereum Institutional wins backing from Standard Chartered and top Ethereum leaders
items.subheadline: Its launch comes as Ethereum's support ecosystem undergoes a broader evolution, following the debut of EthLabs and amid ongoing efforts by the Ethereum Foundation to respond to community criticism over transparency, communication and its role within the ecosystem.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: 5 corruption gaps Congress must close in the Clarity Act
items.subheadline: The most consequential crypto legislation in the world is moving forward in the U.S. Senate. As written, it leaves the United States exposed to money laundering, sanctions evasion, and conflicts of interest at the highest levels of government, argues Greytak.
items.author: Scott Greytak

items.headline: The quantum clock is ticking: it's Bitcoin's problem, not Ethereum's
items.subheadline: A recent research note published by Citi analysts reached a conclusion about quantum risk that should give every institutional bitcoin holder pause, Tabar explains.
items.author: Samir Tabar

items.headline: The U.S. government is betting $2 Billion on quantum computing, and the defense side can't keep up
items.subheadline: Pruden argues that to defend against a quantum computer capable of cryptographically relevant operations, we need post-quantum cryptography and regulatory coordination that the industry has been deferring for years.
items.author: Alex  Pruden

items.headline: Stablecoins Were Meant to Disrupt Finance. Instead, They Became Idle Cash.
items.subheadline: O’Connor argues that crypto’s clearest success story has scaled as money but not as capital.
items.author: John O’Connor

items.headline: If America wants to lead in crypto, it must protect the people who build it
items.subheadline: Despite the Clarity Act’s advancement toward the finish line, there’s one provision under threat for builders that can’t be overlooked, argues Smith.
items.author: Kristin Smith

items.headline: Crypto’s security nightmare won’t be solved by ordinary audits
items.subheadline: Without an update to the current auditing infrastructure, the crypto space will likely continue to suffer significant losses, explains Beyer.
items.author: Stefan Beyer

items.headline: The SEC delayed tokenizing stocks, and here’s why that’s a relief
items.subheadline: Before we rush to tokenize U.S. equities, we need compliance systems that understand context, not just checkboxes. Investors like Michael Burry see the danger, so should the rest of us, urges Chamarajnagar.
items.author: Dr. Ravishankar Chamarajnagar

items.headline: Taiwan passes key crypto law, raising the bar with with licensing, reserve mandates, and tough penalties
items.subheadline: Taiwan has passed a sweeping new law to regulate its crypto sector. Here are key features of the same.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: A political standoff leaves Poland out of the EU's new crypto regulatory roll-out
items.subheadline: President Karol Nawrocki said the new legislation gives regulators excessive powers, sparking concerns of a corporate exodus to neighboring nations.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Europe is rewriting its landmark MiCA regulatory rulebook as hard July 1 deadline passes
items.subheadline: The EU is reviewing whether its landmark crypto regulation MiCA needs updating for a market reshaped by stablecoins and tokenization.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: MiCA is live as Europe's crypto industry splits over winners and losers
items.subheadline: Industry leaders agree regulation is here to stay but disagree over whether MiCA protects consumers, rewards transparency or favors the biggest firms.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Goliath Ventures CEO pleads guilty in $400 million crypto Ponzi case
items.subheadline: Delgado allegedly used investor funds for a lavish lifestyle, including luxury properties and vehicles, while running a fraudulent scheme from 2023 to 2026.
items.author: Francisco Rodrigues

items.headline: Ethereum (ETH) news: Foundation unveils policy guide for governments and institutions
items.subheadline: The Ethereum Foundation said policymakers should distinguish between decentralized public blockchains and networks that remain controlled by corporations or foundations, arguing that governance structures will play a critical role in determining which platforms are suitable for long-term public sector use.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: FBI Director Kash Patel caught sleeping on required disclosure of six-figure MSTR investment
items.subheadline: Patel loaded up on BTC-holder MSTR shares but failed to file a timely disclosure, according to a report by nonpartisan news outlet NOTUS.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: MiCA became law 3 years ago, now Europe's crypto framework is undergoing a rethink
items.subheadline: Europe’s MiCA regime is now up for review, known colloquially as “MiCA 2.0,” through a consultation that closes around September.
items.author: Ian Allison

items.headline: Nasdaq listed Korean Media firm that once wanted to buy 10,000 bitcoin sells all its BTC, pivots to AI
items.subheadline: The Nasdaq-listed Korean media company once lined up $1 billion in financing to buy 10,000 bitcoin. A recent filing confirms its balance is now zero as it pivots to AI infrastructure and fights to stay listed.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: U.S. payroll growth slowed sharply in June, adding only 57,000 jobs
items.subheadline: This morning's data could slow market expectations for a Fed rate hike as soon as this summer or early Fall.
items.author: Stephen Alpher

items.headline: JPMorgan warns Strategy's bitcoin (BTC) sales policy raises crypto market risk
items.subheadline: The bank said Strategy's bitcoin sales policy adds avoidable market uncertainty and should be replaced with equity issuance to build cash reserves.
items.author: Will Canny

items.headline: Ondo debuts SEC-aligned tokenized stock model with BlackRock ETF, Micron shares
items.subheadline: Broadridge and transfer agent Oasis Pro underpin a new structure that keeps tokenized securities within existing U.S. market rules, based on the SEC's third-party custodial model.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: SBI Crypto to shut down mining pool that holds roughly 2% of Bitcoin's hashrate
items.subheadline: Miners must redirect their hashrate before the July 31 cutoff, as the pool will stop accepting contributions after that date.
items.author: Francisco Rodrigues

items.headline: US Treasury sanctions over 100 ISIS-K crypto addresses in latest enforcement action
items.subheadline: ISIS-K allegedly used its media wing to solicit donations via Tron, Monero, and Bitcoin, highlighting stablecoin issuers' growing role in sanctions enforcement.
items.author: Francisco Rodrigues

items.headline: Bitwise says STRC selloff signals crypto market bottom is near
items.subheadline: Bitwise said STRC's volatility reflects a late-cycle leverage unwind, with institutions poised to replace Strategy as bitcoin's biggest buyer.
items.author: Will Canny

items.headline: EToro (ETOR) ramps up blockchain trading push, investing in derivatives venue Extended
items.subheadline: The broker plans to bring perpetual futures into Zengo wallet and expand DeFi products to its core platform as rivals like Robinhood expand onchain offerings.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Securitize (SECZ) takes $295M of its own tokenized stock to Solana, Avalanche amid NYSE debut
items.subheadline: The newly-public company's shares are the largest issuer-sponsored tokenized stock at launch, aiming to make a point against rival third-party stock token issuers.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: BTC price news: Ether, solana extend gains as short squeeze lifts bitcoin to $62,000
items.subheadline: Bearish traders lost $281 million in liquidations over 24 hours, nearly double the longs, as bitcoin pushed to its strongest level in two weeks. Ether is up almost 10% on the week and solana nearly 19% while a rebound in tech stocks eased the pressure from the AI trade.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: XRP price analysis: Bulls test path back toward $1.10 as token zips 4% higher
items.subheadline: Buyers defended a higher low and pushed XRP toward near-term resistance, though the move still needs a clean break above $1.10 to confirm momentum.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Finally. $221 million flow into Bitcoin ETFs, ending a painful 10-day outflow streak
items.subheadline: Spot ETFs had their strongest inflow day in two months, driven by funds other than BlackRock’s IBIT.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Binance pushes back on reports that EU regulators tried to block it
items.subheadline: The exchange denies claims that its financial-crime compliance fell short, calling allegations that it ignored major sanctions concerns categorically false.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Live updates: More bitcoin is now held at a loss than at a profit
items.author: Shaurya Malwa
